Name: Dynamic Adapation
Description: Controls automatic exposure adaptation based on scene luminance. Simulates eye or camera exposure adjustment over time, allowing the scene to dynamically respond to changes in brightness by shifting perceived exposure levels.

Fields.Name: Is Dynamic Adapation Enabled
Fields.Description: Enables or disables dynamic exposure adaptation. When disabled, scene exposure remains fixed regardless of lighting changes.

Fields.Name: Adaptation Speed
Fields.Description: Controls how quickly exposure adapts to changes in scene brightness. Higher values result in faster adjustment.

Fields.Name: Minimum Optimal Brightness
Fields.Description: Defines the lower bound of luminance considered during exposure adaptation.

Fields.Name: Maximum Brightness at Adaptation Level
Fields.Description: Defines the upper luminance limit used for exposure adaptation calculations.

Fields.Name: Adaptation Luminance Center
Fields.Description: Target luminance value that the system adapts toward, acting as the midpoint for exposure balancing.

Fields.Name: Adaptation Sensitivity: Dark
Fields.Description: Controls sensitivity of exposure adaptation in low-light conditions.

Fields.Name: Adaptation Sensitivity: Bright
Fields.Description: Controls sensitivity of exposure adaptation in bright conditions.

Fields.Name: Adaptation Inhibition Value
Fields.Description: Reduces or dampens exposure changes to prevent overreaction to rapid lighting shifts.
